recipes.title: Sauerbraten
recipes.description: German pot roast of beef marinated in wine and vinegar, braised until tender with a tangy gravy.
steps: Combine wine, vinegar, sliced onions, carrots, bay, juniper and cloves.
Submerge beef in marinade and refrigerate 3-4 days, turning daily.
Remove beef, pat dry, strain marinade and reserve liquid and vegetables.
Brown beef in butter on all sides, then add reserved vegetables and cook briefly.
Pour in marinade, cover and braise at 160°C for 3 hours until very tender.
Remove beef, strain sauce, return to pan and crumble in gingerbread cookies; whisk until thick.
Stir in sugar, taste, adjust salt and slice beef to serve with the sauce.

recipes.title: Bratwurst
recipes.description: German pork sausages grilled or pan-fried, served with mustard and bread roll.
steps: Heat oil in a heavy pan over medium heat.
Add bratwurst and cook 12-15 minutes, turning, until deeply browned and cooked through.
Slice onion and sauté in butter until caramelised.
Split bread rolls and warm briefly in the pan.
Place a bratwurst in each roll, top with caramelised onion and a smear of mustard.

recipes.title: Schnitzel
recipes.description: Thin breaded pork or veal cutlet fried until shatteringly crisp and golden.
steps: Pound cutlets between cling film to 5mm thickness; season.
Set up three plates: flour, beaten eggs and breadcrumbs.
Dredge cutlet in flour, dip in egg, then coat in breadcrumbs without pressing.
Heat clarified butter in a wide pan to 170°C.
Fry schnitzel 2-3 minutes per side, swirling pan so it puffs.
Drain briefly on paper, sprinkle with salt and serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Rouladen
recipes.description: German beef rolls stuffed with bacon, onion, mustard and pickle, braised in rich gravy.
steps: Pound beef thin and spread each with mustard.
Lay bacon strips, finely chopped onion and a pickle batter on each piece.
Roll tightly and secure with toothpicks or kitchen string.
Brown rolls in butter on all sides; remove.
Cook chopped onion, carrot and celery in the pan; add tomato paste and flour.
Add wine and stock; return rolls, cover and braise 90 minutes at low heat.
Remove rolls; strain sauce and reduce slightly.
Slice rolls and serve with the gravy and red cabbage.

recipes.title: Spätzle
recipes.description: German egg noodles dropped fresh into boiling water, often crisped with butter or cheese.
steps: Whisk flour, eggs, milk, salt and nutmeg to a smooth, sticky batter; rest 15 minutes.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Press batter through a spätzle maker or coarse colander into the boiling water.
Cook 1-2 minutes until they float, then lift out with a slotted spoon.
Toss with melted butter and parsley before serving.

recipes.title: Kartoffelsalat
recipes.description: German potato salad with a warm vinegar and broth dressing, mustard and bacon.
steps: Boil potatoes in their skins until just tender; cool, peel and slice.
Render diced bacon until crisp; set aside.
Sauté finely chopped onion in the bacon fat until soft.
Pour in broth, vinegar and mustard; simmer 2 minutes.
Whisk in oil and pour warm dressing over the potatoes.
Toss gently, fold in bacon and chives, and serve warm or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Currywurst
recipes.description: Berlin street food: sliced bratwurst smothered in tangy curry-spiced ketchup sauce.
steps: Pan-fry bratwurst in oil until browned and cooked through.
In a separate pan, toast curry powder and paprika in a little oil 30 seconds.
Add ketchup, tomato paste, Worcestershire, sugar and water; simmer 5 minutes.
Slice sausages into rounds.
Pour sauce over the sausage and dust with extra curry powder.
Serve with chips or a bread roll.

recipes.title: Königsberger Klopse
recipes.description: Tender German veal meatballs in a creamy lemon caper sauce.
steps: Soak bread in milk, then squeeze and tear into a bowl.
Combine bread, veal, finely chopped onion, egg, mashed anchovy and salt; knead.
Shape into walnut-sized balls.
Bring broth to a simmer and poach meatballs 12 minutes; remove with slotted spoon.
Melt butter, whisk in flour, then slowly whisk in poaching broth until smooth.
Stir in cream, capers and lemon juice; simmer 5 minutes.
Return meatballs and warm through; serve with boiled potatoes.

recipes.title: Maultaschen
recipes.description: Swabian filled pasta pockets with pork, spinach and onion, served in broth or pan-fried.
steps: Knead flour, eggs and salt into a smooth pasta dough; rest 30 minutes.
Wilt spinach, cool and squeeze dry; chop finely.
Sauté chopped onion until soft; cool.
Soak bread in milk, squeeze, and combine with pork, spinach, onion, egg and nutmeg.
Roll dough thin into long strips.
Pipe filling along one half, fold dough over, press to seal and cut into 6cm squares.
Simmer in broth 8 minutes until they float and pasta is cooked.
Serve in broth or fry slices with onion in butter.

recipes.title: Frikadellen
recipes.description: German pan-fried meat patties of beef and pork seasoned with onion, mustard and herbs.
steps: Soak bread in milk, then squeeze and tear into a bowl.
Sauté finely chopped onion until soft; cool.
Combine meats, bread, onion, egg, mustard, parsley, salt and pepper; knead well.
Shape into 8 thick patties.
Heat oil over medium heat and fry frikadellen 5 minutes per side until cooked through.
Serve with potato salad or bread and mustard.

recipes.title: Eintopf
recipes.description: Hearty German one-pot stew of meat, beans, potatoes and vegetables.
steps: Soak beans overnight; drain.
Cube pork and brown in oil; remove from pot.
Sauté chopped onions and leek until soft.
Return pork, add beans, broth, bay and a little salt; simmer 60 minutes.
Add diced potatoes, carrots and shredded cabbage; cook 25 minutes.
Slice sausage and add for the last 10 minutes; adjust seasoning.

recipes.title: Apfelstrudel
recipes.description: Austrian-German pastry of paper-thin dough wrapped around spiced apples, raisins and breadcrumbs.
steps: Knead flour, water, oil and salt into a smooth, elastic dough; rest 60 minutes covered.
Toast breadcrumbs in 30g butter until golden; cool.
Peel and thinly slice apples; toss with sugar, cinnamon, raisins and lemon juice.
On a floured cloth, stretch dough until paper-thin and translucent.
Brush with melted butter, scatter breadcrumbs, then spread apple mixture along one edge.
Lift the cloth to roll the strudel tightly; transfer to a baking tray seam-down.
Brush with butter and bake at 190°C for 35 minutes until deep golden.
Cool slightly and dust with icing sugar before slicing.
